ground_truth.title: The best tax software of 2025
ground_truth.author: Ana Staples, Alexandria White, Dan Avery
ground_truth.main_content: The best way to make filing your income taxes less painful and time-consuming is to file online. In 2024, close to 40% of taxpayers used tax software or online services to do their taxes. The top tax-prep companies offer both free and paid tiers, along with robust live and AI-powered support and guarantee their accuracy and that they've gotten you the biggest refund possible. Some will even provide you with an interest-free advance on your refund. CNBC Select has run more than a dozen tax software programs through their paces to find the best in a variety of categories. For more on how we made our selections, see our methodology. Best tax software of 2025: Best for ease of use: TurboTax, Best for affordability: TaxSlayer, Best for accuracy guarantee: TaxAct, Best for live support: H&R Block, Best for filing state taxes: Jackson Hewitt, Best for free filing: Cash App Taxes. Best for ease of use: TurboTax. Who's this for? TurboTax guides you through each step of the filing process with straightforward questions and access to explainers, videos and bonus tips. Pricier tiers come with more live support or the option of letting a professional file for you. Standout features: TurboTax guarantees 100% accuracy and the maximum refund possible, audit support for your tax return, plus tax refund advance loans. Online plans: Free, Live Assisted Basic, Business, Deluxe, Premium. Best for affordability: TaxSlayer. Who's this for? TaxSlayer's rates are already lower than those of many competitors, but its packages are priced based on your tax situation and the level of support you want. If you're a confident filer, you can save by skipping features like chat support. Standout features: TaxSlayer has lots of features for self-employed people, including extensive FAQs, reminders about quarterly estimated tax payments and access to a tax pro with self-employed expertise. Online plans: Simply Free, Classic, Premium, Self-Employed, Military. Best accuracy guarantee: TaxAct. Who's this for? Concerned about accuracy? If you're penalized because of a mistake TaxAct made, the company will not only refund the cost of your software and pay the difference in your tax bill, it will reimburse any penalties or interest and cover legal or audit fees up to $100,000. Standout features: TaxAct's Deduction Maximizer tool can suggest deduction options based on your situation. Online plans: Free, Deluxe, Premier, Self-Employed. Best for live support: H&R Block. Who's this for? Need help when you file? Real-time support from H&R Block's AI Tax Assist feature, online chat with a tax professional and Worry-Free Audit Support are available with all paid tiers. H&R Block also has thousands of offices nationwide for in-person support. Standout benefits: H&R Block's free tier lets you file both federal and state simple returns for free with no income restrictions. It covers situations many competitors don't, including unemployment insurance and deductions for education costs. Online plans: Free, Deluxe, Premium, Self-Employed. Best for filing state taxes: Jackson Hewitt. Who's this for? Jackson Hewitt charges a flat $25 fee for DIY federal and state returns — no matter how many states you need to file. Other operations can charge upwards for $40 for each state filing. Standout benefits: Like pricier operations, Jackson Hewitt offers free informational audit assistance and accuracy and maximum refund guarantees. Online plans: $25 e-file plan, regardless of the complexity of your return. It does not import tax documents from competitors and other sources. Best for free filing: Cash App Taxes. Who's this for? Straightforward and free, Cash App Taxes is best for DIY filers who know what they're doing. You can file state and federal returns for free with access to nearly every IRS form and schedule, although there's no real professional support and multistate returns aren't supported. Standout features: Cash App Taxes includes free audit defense with every return. Online plans: Free. What is tax software and how does it work? There are three main ways to file a tax return: A paper return sent through the mail, working with a tax professional or using tax-prep software. Commercial tax-prep software programs are the preferred method for 44% of tax filers. They allow you to go at your own pace anywhere you can go online, with step-by-step guidance and access to professional support. The IRS has launched Direct File, a free web-based platform that allows taxpayers in 24 states to file their returns online. Direct File is only available for simple returns, however, and there are income limits. In addition, there's not much in the way of live support. Since most tax software companies offer a variety of packages, you'll need to decide which tier is right for you. That will determine both the forms you need to fill out and the price you'll pay. Once you make your selection, you'll be prompted to answer questions about your finances for the year and upload relevant tax forms. Most companies will offer to file your return to the IRS or state revenue department on your behalf. If you got an early start, the system may have to wait until tax filing season officially begins. You should also be able to arrange your tax refund through the software company and, in some cases, deduct the cost of their services for a fee. What is a simple tax return? Simple tax returns are the most basic type of return you can file. Software companies vary in their criteria, but a simple return is generally prepared using IRS Form 1040 and is limited to: W-2 income, Limited interest and dividend income, Standard deduction, Unemployment income. Some tax filing programs will include limited credits as part of a simple return, including the Earned Income Tax Credit, child tax credits and student loan/education deductions. How to choose the best tax software. There are a lot of choices for tax-prep software, both cloud-based and desktop. Here's what you should look for when deciding on the package that works for you. Ease of use: Affordability is important but if the platform is confusing or frustrating, it's not worth saving a few bucks. Is the interface intuitive? Can you upload previous years' tax documents? Are you guided through the process with straightforward questions? See if there's a walkthrough or a free trial to give it a test drive. Read customer reviews both on the website and on third-party sites like Trustpilot. Supported tax forms: Make sure the software you choose supports all the forms you need. If you're self-employed, you'll need to file a Schedule C. If you have investment income, you'll need a Schedule D. If you have a mortgage, you'll need a Schedule A. Most companies list the forms they support on their websites. If you're not sure, contact customer service. Guarantees: Most tax-prep companies guarantee their work. That means if they make a mistake, they'll pay any penalties and interest you incur. Some also guarantee you'll get the maximum refund possible. Read the fine print to see what's covered and what's not. Support: If you run into trouble, you'll want to be able to get help. Look for companies that offer live chat, phone support or even in-person assistance. Some companies also offer audit support, which can be invaluable if you're ever audited by the IRS. Price: Tax software can range from free to several hundred dollars. The price usually depends on the complexity of your return and the level of support you need. If you have a simple return, you may be able to file for free. If you have a more complex return, you'll likely need to pay for a premium package. Methodology: To determine the best tax software, CNBC Select analyzed and compared tax software from over a dozen companies. We considered each company's pricing, features, ease of use, customer support and guarantees. We also looked at customer reviews and ratings from third-party sites like Trustpilot. We then narrowed down our list to the top six companies in a variety of categories.
with: The best way to make filing your income taxes less painful and time-consuming is to file online.
CNBC Select has run more than a dozen tax software programs through their paces to find the best in a variety of categories.
Best for ease of use: TurboTax guides you through each step of the filing process with straightforward questions and access to explainers, videos and bonus tips.
Best for affordability: TaxSlayer's rates are already lower than those of many competitors, but its packages are priced based on your tax situation and the level of support you want.

Commercial tax-prep software programs are the preferred method for 44% of tax filers. They allow you to go at your own pace anywhere you can go online, with step-by-step guidance and access to professional support.
